hero.title: Browser PDF Reader
hero.description: Open local PDFs in the browser and save lightweight reading-session metadata without uploading documents.

features.title: Private reading for local PDF files
features.description: Browser PDF Reader V1 focuses on explicit file selection, local viewing, and small browser-only reading sessions.

items.title: Open local PDFs
items.description: Use the browser file picker to open a PDF from the current device.

items.title: Track reading position
items.description: Save document name, page count, last viewed page, and zoom level locally.

items.title: Reopen intentionally
items.description: Return to a saved session by selecting the local PDF again.

flow.title: Open -> Read -> Resume
steps: Select a local PDF from the workspace.
Review page count, navigate pages, and adjust zoom.
Save a metadata-only reading session in the browser.
Reopen saved sessions by reselecting the local PDF file.

benefits.title: Review documents without giving up file control
benefits.description: V1 avoids backend storage and cloud synchronization so sensitive PDFs remain on the user's device.

items.title: Privacy-first
items.description: PDF binary content is not stored in localStorage and is never sent to Ansiversa servers.

items.title: Fast resume
items.description: Saved sessions remember where the user stopped reading.

items.title: Easy cleanup
items.description: Users can delete individual sessions or clear all local reading history.

differences.title: A reader, not a document cloud
differences.description: Browser PDF Reader keeps V1 intentionally narrow: local viewing, metadata history, and local insights.

items.title: Open locally
items.description: Files enter the workflow only through an explicit browser file picker action.

items.title: Save metadata
items.description: The app stores reading-session fields, not PDF files.

items.title: Resume safely
items.description: Reopening asks the user to reselect the same local file instead of syncing documents.

feel.title: A focused local PDF desk
feel.description: The workflow keeps reading controls, saved sessions, and local insights close together.

items.title: Choose deliberately
items.description: The file picker is always user initiated.

items.title: Read without setup
items.description: Native browser PDF support handles the viewing experience.

items.title: Return with context
items.description: History restores page and zoom after the user reselects the PDF.

useCases.title: Useful for sensitive reading sessions
useCases.description: Browser PDF Reader is best when users want a quick local reader with lightweight session memory.

items.title: Study PDFs
items.description: Resume long readings without uploading class material.

items.title: Work documents
items.description: Open local briefs, contracts, or reports while keeping files off the backend.

items.title: Personal records
items.description: Review private documents with metadata-only history.

audiences.title: Who uses Browser PDF Reader
audiences.description: Helpful for people who need local document review without cloud document storage.

items.title: Students
items.description: Track reading position across study documents.

items.title: Professionals
items.description: Review local PDFs without sending files to a server.

items.title: Privacy-conscious users
items.description: Keep sensitive PDF content on the device.

resourceSection.title: Browser PDF Reader essentials
resourceSection.description: The V1 workflow is intentionally browser-local and metadata-only.

items.title: Workspace
items.description: Select a PDF, view it, navigate pages, and set zoom.

items.title: History
items.description: Search, edit, reopen, delete, or clear local reading sessions.

groupedSection.title: Read locally without backend document storage
groupedSection.description: Move from explicit file selection to metadata-only session recall.

items.name: File picker
items.description: Choose a PDF from the current device.

items.name: Reader controls
items.description: Move between pages and adjust zoom.

technicalSection.title: Browser-local V1 implementation
technicalSection.description: Browser PDF Reader follows the shared Ansiversa mini-app architecture while avoiding backend document handling.
items: Lazy-loaded frontend module with shared overview, authenticated page state, page header, form drawer, empty state, feedback, record action, and stats components.
No PDF upload, PDF binary localStorage, OCR, AI analysis, annotation sync, cloud storage, or new PDF SDK dependency.

items.question: Does Browser PDF Reader upload my PDF?
items.answer: No. V1 opens PDFs through the browser and does not upload file content to Ansiversa servers.

items.question: What is saved locally?
items.answer: Only reading-session metadata: document name, page count, last viewed page, zoom level, and timestamps.

items.question: Can I reopen a saved session without selecting the file again?
items.answer: No. Because the PDF file is not stored, reopening asks you to select the local PDF again.

items.question: Does V1 include OCR, AI summaries, or annotations?
items.answer: No. Those are intentionally outside the V1 browser-local scope.

finalCta.title: Explore private browser-local PDF reading
finalCta.description: Open a local PDF, save your reading position, and keep the file on your device.
